title: Tangy Sweet Algerian Knafeh Bowl
description: A romantic bowl of golden knafeh layered with sweet cheese, almond‑yogurt sauce, and fragrant tadka, finished with a splash of rose water—low‑sodium, tender, and utterly irresistible.

ingredients.name: Tadka mix
ingredients.amount: 1 tsp oil, ¼ tsp cumin seeds, pinch dried red chili, ⅛ tsp black salt

instructions: Preheat the oven to 180 °C (356 °F). Arrange the shredded phyllo in a shallow baking dish and lightly drizzle with ghee, then bake for 12–15 minutes until golden and crisp.
While the phyllo bakes, combine mozzarella, ricotta, sugar, lemon zest, cardamom, cinnamon, and a pinch of black salt in a bowl to create the sweet cheese filling.
Once the phyllo is golden, spread the cheese mixture evenly over it, then sprinkle toasted almonds on top.
Return the assembled dish to the oven for an additional 8–10 minutes, or until the cheese melts and the edges turn a deep amber.
In a small bowl whisk the yogurt, rose water, a pinch of black salt, and a pinch of dried chili until smooth, setting aside for later use.
Heat a teaspoon of oil in a small skillet, add cumin seeds and let them pop, then stir in the dried chili and a splash of rose water to create the tadka.
Remove the knafeh from the oven and drizzle the yogurt sauce over the top, followed by a generous pour of the tadka.
Ladle the warm knafeh into dessert bowls, garnish with extra toasted almonds and a light dusting of cinnamon if desired.
Serve immediately, allowing the aromatic rose water to mingle with the sweet‑tangy flavors.
Allow the bowl to cool slightly before eating to enjoy the full spectrum of textures and aromas.
